description: Controlled cold-cache baseline of the signed v1.1.0 packaged executable against the user's exact 4,479.9-second lecture. The package ran from a fresh extraction path containing spaces and wrote only to an isolated data directory outside LecturePackData.

media.title: CL100 - Day 3 - Mesopotamia and Babylon
media.detector_preset_note: The v1.1 packaged acceptance driver creates new jobs with the Balanced detector. The user's existing normal-use job used Detailed; this controlled limitation is recorded rather than silently treating the settings as identical.

evidence: The monitored child process executable was the package's bin/vulkan/whisper-cli.exe.
Its command line used ggml-small.en-q8_0.bin and the isolated job audio WAV.
Windows GPU Engine counters attributed to the process tree peaked at 86.62 percent.

timing_notes.alignment_export: v1.1 executes ExportService.align_and_export inside its Align stage, so separate alignment and export timings cannot be recovered from the signed package.
timing_notes.context_repair: The acceptance driver ran deterministic name-based Context Repair after pipeline_completed but did not time that operation separately. Ollama was not exercised in this baseline.
timing_notes.model_backend_load: v1.1 includes model/backend load inside the Transcribe stage and exposes no separate timestamp in the packaged acceptance report.
timing_notes.overlap: Transcribe and Detect Slides overlapped. Their stage work totals 1,128.88 seconds, while the required pipeline completed in 619.31 seconds; the critical parallel branch was transcription at 605.41 seconds.
timing_notes.optional_ai_blocking: pipeline_completed was true before the deterministic Context Repair acceptance round began, so optional enrichment did not block the usable-job completion signal in this driver.

gpu_engine_percent_sum.note: Sum of Windows GPU Engine utilization counters attributed to the monitored process tree, sampled every ten seconds.

automated_tests.preliminary_harness_timeout_note: The first invocation used a 120-second shell limit and was terminated at 87 percent with all executed tests passing. No pytest process remained. The entire suite was then rerun from the beginning with a five-minute shell limit and passed.

user_normal_use_reference.note: The existing job confirms the user's real configuration and completed state but does not contain authoritative per-stage timestamps, CPU/GPU samples, or peak RAM. The controlled packaged run is therefore the performance baseline.

known_v1_1_telemetry_gaps: No separate alignment and export durations.
No separately timed Context Repair or Ollama duration.
The packaged driver does not emit stage-cache hit signals into its report.
The packaged driver does not persist backend_info signal text; actual backend is proven by the monitored child executable path and GPU counters.
